channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Where Does Madagascar’s Gen Z-Led Movement Stand Now?
item.description: The country has announced a constitutional referendum and elections. Can that bring democratic change?

item.title: Both Trump and Xi Overestimate Themselves
item.description: Elites in both China and the U.S. are too easily tempted to buy into their own myths.

item.title: Big tech’s fat profits conceal unsettling cashflows
item.description: Five cloud giants are sacrificing their returns to prop up the AI boom

item.title: Trump Has One Major Focus While in China: Trade
item.description: The U.S. president is expected to downplay other issues to secure new trade deals.

item.title: Trump, Xi Prepare for High-Stakes Summit in Beijing
item.description: The U.S. president is expected to downplay other issues to secure new trade deals.

item.title: India Still Stands By BRICS
item.description: The group’s foreign ministers convene in New Delhi amid global tensions.

item.title: The war between businesses and hackers enters a perilous new era
item.description: AI agents present novel dangers

item.title: The world’s best-sounding nightclub is in an unexpected place
item.description: Why DJs are flocking to an obscure town in west Germany

item.title: Is AI putting graduates out of work already?
item.description: If you are studying coding, we might have some bad news

item.title: There’s No Need to Fear China’s Economy
item.description: Beijing can’t easily afford to escalate any economic struggle with Washington.

item.title: AI models are being used to predict conflict
item.description: Good data are hard to come by

item.title: Neanderthals went to the dentist (really)
item.description: They did so tens of thousands of years before humans

item.title: Why Congress Can’t Stop Trump’s Iran War
item.description: A half-century of messy politics has inverted the Constitution’s design—and there’s no easy fix.

item.title: Donald Trump’s midterm strategy: purge the Republican Party
item.description: A case study in two bitter primaries for safe Republican seats

item.title: Is It Really a ‘Cease-Fire’ if Both Sides Are Still Shooting?
item.description: A mediation expert explains why the U.S.-Iran cease-fire is so vulnerable.

item.title: Europe Needs the Digital Euro
item.description: Although the European Union has transformed the continent politically and economically, many citizens perceive integration as a source of regulations and constraints rather than as something that improves their daily lives. The digital euro could change that, but only if policymakers are willing to make the political case for it.

item.title: Russia and the China-US Summit
item.description: The upcoming meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and Xi will be followed by a Putin-Xi summit. Will there be scope for triangular diplomacy?

item.title: Why Hasn’t China Criticized Australia’s National Defense Strategy?
item.description: Unlike in 2024, China sees an opportunity to influence Australia. That has led Beijing to refrain from criticism.

item.title: Mali Plays Russian Roulette
item.description: Recent violence exposes the flaws in the Kremlin’s mercenary security model.

item.title: Japan’s Middle Power Arms Strategy in the Indo-Pacific
item.description: Tokyo’s defense exports are becoming tools for building a new middle power security network across the Indo-Pacific.

item.title: Trump’s Lasting Mark
item.description: Trump’s planned White House ballroom and other architectural projects are not distractions but part of a broader effort to inscribe his preferred values and view of America into institutions that future presidents will be forced to operate within. Resisting it will require organized refusal now and institutional redesign later.

item.title: China’s 50,000-Youth Initiative and the New Politics of China-US Exchange
item.description: Sponsored student visits are keeping international contact alive, but schools and analysts should ask questions about the intentions behind the visits as well as the impressions they create.

item.title: China’s Mahan: The Man Who Created China’s Modern Navy
item.description: Insights from Xiaobing Li.

item.title: Beijing’s Quest for Uniformity May Be Its Achilles’ Heel
item.description: Forced assimilation is costing China dearly.

item.title: Beijing’s Quest for Uniformity May Be Its Achilles’ Heel
item.description: Forced assimilation is costing China dearly.

item.title: War, Pandemics, and the Struggle for Healthcare Security
item.description: Countries must prepare for the dual threat of pandemics and supply chain shocks.

item.title: The Military Imperative for OPCON Transfer
item.description: Military organizations must contend with operational realities, not political declarations. That's exactly why OPCON transfer is necessary.

item.title: America’s Autism Obsession
item.description: The American public’s fascination with neurodivergence has increased significantly in recent years. One possibility for this newfound obsession is that autism has become an interpretive screen onto which anxieties about technology, identity, and the future are projected.

item.title: Why Violence Persists in Nigeria
item.description: And how governance reform can break the cycle.

item.title: One Country, Two Islands: Beijing’s Long Bet That an Open Hainan Can Hook Taiwan
item.description: Hainan is today engaged in what may be China’s most radical economic experiment since the 1980s – and Taiwan is part of the rationale.

item.title: Tashiev Indicted for Coup Plot, Case Classified
item.description: According to his lawyer, Tashiev has been indicted on charges of plotting a coup and the case has been classified.

item.title: The War on Billionaires Is Dangerous Nonsense
item.description: The left- and right-wing populists calling for targeted taxes on the ultra-wealthy argue that the economic game is rigged against typical workers, allowing the rich to take from the middle class and the poor. But this claim is untrue—and the opposite of what aspiring young people need to hear.

item.title: How Russia Lost Friends and Global Influence
item.description: Far from restoring Russia’s great-power status, the Ukraine war has left Russia stretched so thin that it has repeatedly failed to fulfill its commitments to partners and allies. With even Russia's closest friends hedging their bets, the Kremlin's ability to project power and shape world affairs has been severely weakened.

item.title: PS Events: Economics of AI
Gulf Energy Crisis Exposes Southeast Asia’s Renewable Energy Dilemma
item.description: Will the current oil supply shock hold back the region’s green transition, or accelerate it?

item.title: India’s Role in a Disordered World
item.description: Diplomacy of the highest quality is more essential than ever.

item.title: The Promise and Peril of U.S.-China Summitry
item.description: What Xi wants from Trump—and Trump might get from Xi.

item.title: America Has Lost Its Leverage Over China
item.description: How Trump and Xi could cement Beijing’s advantage for years to come.

item.title: The Geopolitics of the Trump-Xi Meeting
item.description: Amid the expected pomp and circumstance, what can we expect from the Trump-Xi summit?

item.title: How the world has avoided an oil catastrophe so far
item.description: The great commodity-market mystery is deepening

item.title: The Thorium Gamble: India’s Nuclear Breakthrough
item.description: Dr. Anil Kakodkar, former chair of India's Atomic Energy Commission, joins the podcast to discuss the history and future of India’s three-stage nuclear program.

item.title: Modi Calls on Indians to Consume Less Petrol, Postpone Travel Abroad. He Isn’t Leading by Example
item.description: The austerity measures are aimed at dealing with oil and gas shortages and conserving foreign exchange.
